Observing system behavior. Almost all living systems involve the regulation of many genes and a complex network of interactions among their expressed products. The ability to observe these systems as systems and at a level of resolution relevant to the systems themselves is a recent technical advance. Here, a small section of a mRNA chip shows gene expression levels in yeast. Each row on the chip depicts the expression level of a different gene under different experimental conditions (columns). Red and green spots show high and low levels of expression, respectively. Image courtesy of Alejandro Colman-Lerner.
